title: clap -- Command Line Argument Parser
description: The most popular CLI argument parsing crate for Rust. Clap provides a derive-based API for defining CLI arguments as structs, with automatic help generation, validation, subcommands, and shell completions.

commonPatterns.title: Subcommands
Positional arguments and flags
Value enums for constrained choices

whenToUse: Any time you are building a command-line tool and need to parse arguments, flags, subcommands, or generate --help output.
alternatives: argh (simpler, from Google), pico-args (minimal, zero dependencies), bpaf (composable, derive + builder). For TUI apps: clap handles args, then use ratatui for the terminal UI.
